{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01XzBcsrg5Nu8aYeKm7zyMjg","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The user wants me to provide a clinical pharmacogenomics recommendation for ribostamycin (an aminoglycoside antibiotic) in a patient with MT-RNR1 genotype associated with increased risk of aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss.\n\nLet me think about this:\n\n**MT-RNR1 and Aminoglycoside-Induced Ototoxicity:**\n\nThe MT-RNR1 gene encodes the mitochondrial 12S ribosomal RNA. Certain variants in MT-RNR1 (most notably m.1555A>G and m.1494C>T) are associated with hypersensitivity to aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss (ototoxicity). These are mitochondrial variants.\n\n\nThe m.1555A>G variant is particularly well-characterized—it alters the mitochondrial ribosome to resemble bacterial ribosomes more closely, making cells vulnerable to aminoglycoside toxicity even at standard doses, potentially causing permanent sensorineural hearing loss. Ribostamycin, like all aminoglycosides, poses a significant ototoxic risk in patients carrying this variant.\n\nFor anyone with MT-RNR1 risk variants, the clinical consensus is clear: aminoglycosides should be avoided whenever possible. If they're absolutely necessary, the approach is to use the minimum effective dose for the shortest timeframe with close monitoring. Major guidelines including CPIC strongly recommend against aminoglycoside use in these patients due to the dramatically elevated risk of irreversible hearing damage. The MT-RNR1 variants structurally mimic the bacterial ribosomal target, making cochlear hair cells exceptionally vulnerable to aminoglycoside toxicity and resulting in permanent hearing loss even at standard doses.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1194,"cache_read_input_tokens":18420,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1194,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":0,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"73f289fb-909f-4aa1-b851-06364e8077a2","uuid":"3b0af5ad-5f1b-451f-a5b9-b8166900d83d"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01XzBcsrg5Nu8aYeKm7zyMjg","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01R4YM7ZiToc7fD6qiRwjRLU","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat > /app/answers.json <<'JSON'\n{\n  \"recommendation\": \"Avoid ribostamycin and all aminoglycoside antibiotics if clinically feasible.
